metadata.note: Pan-PPAR agonists activate all three PPAR subtypes (α, β/δ, γ) for metabolic diseases (NASH/MASH, diabetes, dyslipidemia). 1 marketed, 2 clinical, 1 discontinued, 6 preclinical.

chemical_identifiers.notes: First clinically tested pan-PPAR activator. Historically classified as fibrate (primarily PPARα), but pan-PPAR activity (α, β/δ, γ) confirmed in multiple studies.

pubmed_papers.title: Dual and pan-peroxisome proliferator-activated receptor co-activation in metabolic disorders

compounds.key_findings: First clinically tested pan-PPAR activator stimulating PPAR-α, PPAR-β/δ, and PPAR-γ. Marketed globally since 1978 for hypertriglyceridemia and mixed dyslipidemia. Clinical data show increases in HDL-C, reductions in triglycerides, improved insulin sensitivity, and reduced cardiovascular events and incident diabetes in metabolic syndrome patients. Pan-PPAR activity was recognized retrospectively.
compounds.notes: Marketed since 1978. Brand: Bezalip. First clinically tested pan-PPAR.

chemical_identifiers.notes: Pan-PPAR agonist with moderate, well-balanced profile (fuller activation of α and δ, partial γ).

clinical_trials.note: Phase 3 trial in MASH with fibrosis. Multiple Phase 2 studies completed.

pubmed_papers.title: A Randomized, Controlled Trial of the Pan-PPAR Agonist Lanifibranor in NASH
The pan-PPAR agonist lanifibranor improves cardiometabolic health in patients with MASH

compounds.key_findings: Most advanced pan-PPAR agonist in development. Phase 3 NATiV3 trial for MASH with fibrosis. Repeatedly described as pan-PPAR agonist activating all three isoforms with moderate, well-balanced profile (fuller activation of α and δ, partial γ). Improves insulin sensitivity, lipids, steatohepatitis, liver fibrosis, portal hypertension and broader cardiometabolic endpoints in clinical and preclinical data. Only clearly pan-PPAR agent in late-stage development for liver disease.
compounds.notes: Phase 3 NATiV3. Most advanced pan-PPAR for MASH. NEJM 2021, Nat Commun 2024.

chemical_identifiers.notes: Non-TZD structured PPAR modulator. PPAR pan-agonist with activation of PPAR-α, PPAR-δ, and PPAR-γ.

clinical_trials.note: Non-inferior HbA1c reduction (~-1.4% at 24 weeks) vs sitagliptin. Greater improvements in fasting/postprandial glucose and fasting insulin than sitagliptin.

pubmed_papers.title: Efficacy and Safety of Chiglitazar, a Novel Peroxisome Proliferator–Activated Receptor Pan-Agonist
Chiglitazar, a PPAR pan-agonist: impacts on type 2 diabetes

compounds.key_findings: PPAR pan-agonist with activation of PPAR-α, PPAR-δ, and PPAR-γ. Multiple Phase 3 trials completed for T2DM. Phase 3 vs sitagliptin: non-inferior HbA1c reduction (~-1.4% at 24 weeks), with greater improvements in fasting and postprandial glucose and fasting insulin than sitagliptin. Being explored in MASLD/MASH with T2DM. Real-world and mechanistic studies emphasize balanced, triple-isoform activity with favorable metabolic effects.
compounds.notes: Phase 3 completed. T2DM indication. Balanced triple-isoform activity. PMID: 36654287.

chemical_identifiers.notes: Rationally designed pan-PPAR-active ligand with EC₅₀ values of ~0.85–1.3 µM for PPAR-α/δ/γ.

clinical_trials.note: Phase 2 diabetes program stopped due to safety and benefit-risk concerns, in line with broader setbacks for glitazar-type drugs.

pubmed_papers.title: Scaffold-based discovery of indeglitazar, a PPAR pan-active anti-diabetic agent

compounds.key_findings: Rationally designed pan-PPAR-active ligand with EC₅₀ values of ~0.85–1.3 µM for PPAR-α/δ/γ. Advanced to Phase 2 clinical trials for T2DM, then development was discontinued. Structural studies show indeglitazar bound to each subtype's ligand-binding domain, confirming true pan-agonist binding. Less adipogenic than full PPAR-γ agonists, only partially up-regulates adiponectin, but still improves glucose, triglycerides, cholesterol, and weight in animal models. Phase 2 program stopped due to safety and benefit-risk concerns.
compounds.notes: DISCONTINUED. Phase 2 completed. Pfizer. Safety/benefit-risk concerns. PNAS 2009.

chemical_identifiers.notes: Synthetic small-molecule PPAR ligand. Identified as strongest pan-PPAR agonist among derivative series.

pubmed_papers.title: Physiological characterization of a novel PPAR pan agonist, MHY2013

compounds.key_findings: Identified as strongest pan-PPAR agonist among derivative series, activating all three PPARs in PPRE-luciferase assays to levels comparable with WY-14643 (α), GW501516 (δ), and rosiglitazone (γ). In db/db mice, oral MHY2013 improved insulin resistance, dyslipidemia, and hepatic steatosis without weight gain and without hepatic/renal toxicity at study doses. Mechanistically increased FGF21 and adiponectin, promoted adipose browning, and enhanced fatty-acid oxidation in liver, adipose and muscle.
compounds.notes: Preclinical. Strongest pan-PPAR in series. db/db mice efficacy. FGF21/adiponectin elevation.

chemical_identifiers.notes: Hybrid of fibrate pharmacophore and resveratrol scaffold. EC₅₀: 1.75 µM (α), 19.2 µM (δ), 25.3 µM (γ).

pubmed_papers.title: Novel PPAR pan agonist, ZBH ameliorates hyperlipidemia and insulin resistance

compounds.key_findings: Hybrid of fibrate pharmacophore and resveratrol scaffold. Directly binds PPAR-α, PPAR-δ and PPAR-γ ligand-binding domains in SPR assays; KD values comparable to or better than bezafibrate. Cell-based transactivation: EC₅₀ ≈ 1.75 µM (α), 19.2 µM (δ), 25.3 µM (γ), much more potent than bezafibrate, especially at PPAR-α. In hyperlipidemic hamsters, 5-week treatment significantly reduced TG, TC, LDL-C, FFA and hyperinsulinemia, improved insulin sensitivity more than bezafibrate, without worsening liver or muscle enzymes. Also up-regulated SIRT1 and multiple fatty-acid oxidation genes in liver and muscle.
compounds.notes: Preclinical. Fibrate-resveratrol hybrid. EC₅₀: 1.75/19.2/25.3 µM (α/δ/γ). SIRT1 activation.

chemical_identifiers.notes: Phenylpropionic-acid-type ligand. EC₅₀: 61 nM (α), 120 nM (δ), 43 nM (γ) - very high potency.

compounds.key_findings: Phenylpropionic-acid-type ligand sold as research reagent. Explicitly described as pan agonist that activates all three PPAR subtypes. In biochemical assays: EC₅₀ 61 nM (α), 120 nM (δ), 43 nM (γ) - very high potency across all isoforms. Used as positive control for PPAR assays and in structural biology work to study how a single ligand can adapt to different LBD shapes of PPAR-α, -δ, and -γ.
compounds.notes: Research reagent. Tool compound. EC₅₀: 61/120/43 nM (α/δ/γ). Positive control for PPAR assays.

chemical_identifiers.notes: Prenylated benzopyran derivative. Strong PPAR-α, moderate PPAR-β/δ, weak but present PPAR-γ activity.

pubmed_papers.title: Anti-inflammatory effects and improved metabolic derangements in ob/ob mice by BP-2

compounds.key_findings: Newly synthesized prenylated benzopyran with pan-PPAR activity. Transactivation profile: strong PPAR-α, moderate PPAR-β/δ, weak but present PPAR-γ activity. In endothelial cells, decreased TNF-α-induced ICAM-1, VCAM-1, and fractalkine, reduced p38-MAPK/NF-κB activation via PPARβ/δ-RXRα interactions. In ob/ob mice, improved glucose and triglycerides, reduced liver and adipose inflammation, shifted macrophages toward M2-like phenotype (↑CD206).
compounds.notes: Preclinical lead. Prenylated benzopyran. Strong α, moderate β/δ, weak γ. PMID: 36586645.

chemical_identifiers.notes: Natural glycoside of resveratrol. Identified as top pan-PPAR ligand via high-throughput virtual screening.

pubmed_papers.title: In silico and in vitro investigations reveal pan-PPAR agonist activity and anti-NAFLD efficacy of Polydatin

compounds.key_findings: Natural glycoside of resveratrol. High-throughput virtual screening against all three PPAR LBDs identified Polydatin as top pan-PPAR ligand, with balanced Glide scores and stable MD simulations in PPAR-α, -β/δ, and -γ. Nuclear pan-PPAR DNA-binding assays in steatotic HepG2 cells showed marked increases in PPAR-β/δ and PPAR-γ activity and PPAR-α at least comparable to reference hepatoprotective agent. Increased protein levels of PPAR-α, -β/δ, and -γ, activated AMPK, decreased ACC1 and FASN, increased CPT1α, and up-regulated GLUT2. Reduced lipid accumulation (Oil Red O), ROS, MDA and ALT in OA-induced steatotic cells more effectively than silymarin.
compounds.notes: Preclinical. Natural resveratrol glycoside. HTVS-identified pan-PPAR. Anti-NAFLD efficacy.

chemical_identifiers.notes: Prenylated isoflavone. EC₅₀: 0.74 µM (γ), 4.00 µM (α), 8.07 µM (β/δ) in 293T cells.

pubmed_papers.title: Development of heterocyclic PPAR ligands for potential therapeutic applications - Bavachinin section

compounds.key_findings: Prenylated isoflavone from Psoralea corylifolia. Novel natural pan-PPAR agonist with EC₅₀ values in 293T cells of 0.74 µM (PPAR-γ), 4.00 µM (PPAR-α), 8.07 µM (PPAR-β/δ). SAR work shows specific A-ring substitutions (C-6 isopentenyl, C-7 methoxy) and B-ring modifications tune pan-PPAR potency. Bavachinin analog (compound 41) has even higher α/β activity while maintaining γ potency, noted as more powerful pan-PPAR analog.
compounds.notes: Preclinical. Natural prenylated isoflavone. EC₅₀: 0.74/4.00/8.07 µM (γ/α/β/δ). SAR optimized.
